title: More functionality for the broadcast view
excerpt: Continuing the discussion from Broadcasting Dashboards:

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: Continuing the discussion from Broadcasting Dashboards:

posts.author: tara (tara)
posts.cookedHtml: would love to have the ability to hide columns on the broadcast view. Just like you can hide from certain team members, would like to be able to choose the option to show or hide on broadcast view.

posts.author: Creative_Mamm_Al (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml:
Speaking of broadcast views…

Is there a way to broadcast only the calendar view? I feel like I’ve done this before but now can’t seem to find how.

For reference: when sharing a project timeline, I’d rather just share with them the calendar view as opposed to the main table version.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Creative_Mamm_Al

Right now you can just broadcast the table view.

We’ll pass along this feedback though - thanks for sharing

posts.author: Dan_nura (Dan Medcraft)
posts.cookedHtml:
just giving this thread a little bump rather than create a new thread…

I’d love to see some added functionality:

  • be able to exclude certain groups from displaying
  • be able to exclude certain columns from displaying
  • be able to whitelist it (or at the very least, remove the messaging in the top right corner)
  • make the borders slightly thinner

posts.author: Naomi (Naomi Brooks)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree, we would love to use this to share some but not all info with our customers.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Naomi and Dan!

We’re considering these options for controlling what data displays on the board. Stay tuned for updates!!

posts.author: KamilMark (Kamil Mark)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
is this bug or linked columns doesn’t work in broadcast? This is critical…

Also, any ETA on “controlling what data displays on the board”?

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @KamilMark

Right now, mirrored columns won’t show in the broadcast view.

Regarding the ability to control what data shows on broadcast, right now, this isn’t being worked on but we are still considering adding this functionality in the future.

Thanks

posts.author: Danny (DIGITAL STEAM)
posts.cookedHtml:
As a user, I would really appreciate the capability to format broadcast table layouts with my own CSS/framework.

For example; currently, the broadcast posts as a table with it’s own styling framework. Why not allow admins to apply other layouts and even apply it’s own CSS framework. We use UIKIT in all our solutions and allowing site admins to embed their own styles would allow for style continuity/coherence.

There truly is a LOT once can do with Monday.com’s broadcasting. It can literally be used as a customizable datasource! I see unlimited capabilities here! I could build an intranet on it and embed that into our websites

Thanks for considering
Danny

posts.author: olivervaagen (Oliver Vaagen)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to be able to create more than one broadcast views with different filters set on them. Or at least create the broadcast view from not the main board view.

posts.author: mdowell (Mitch Dowell)
posts.cookedHtml:
Not sure if this is currently possible, but here is a scenario that we are curious about: Our Marketing team keeps a Monday board for our email nurture streams that we have running in Marketo that captures various criteria for the various streams (<-- simple table content, nothing sophisticated). However, we have a Files column where we include the latest jpg screenshot of the email. We were hoping to broadcast this board so our remote sales teams can not only view the criteria, but also click on the thumbnail to view the latest screenshot. However, it looks like the jpg files are not clickable in broadcast mode.

If this is doable, and we’re missing something, please let me know Otherwise, if this functionality is not available in broadcast mode, I would certainly like to recommend it for future consideration.

Thanks!

posts.author: Danny (DIGITAL STEAM)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi

I was wondering if there was any development plans in your road-map to improve the shared board embed views/options ?

For us, Monday.com is pivotal in managing projects and customer data. Having the ability to share some of this data through our client zone (in our website) is becoming just as important. The embed option allows us this flexibility. All that’s missing is better viewing options (layouts, styling …)

I look forward to hearing your response about this.

Danny

posts.author: Danny (DIGITAL STEAM)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Tara
I suggest you “vote” for this up on top here (way on top).
This, I presume, helps Monday.com in deciding what to do next
Danny

posts.author: tara (tara)
posts.cookedHtml: I did not know that you could vote on them - thanks! Although I don’t think 2 votes is going to get anything.

posts.author: rick (Rick Castorani)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Julia-monday.com this is following up from my previous post here: Broadcasting Dashboards - #10 by rick

The new sharing feature was released for Dashboards but it did not include the ability to broadcast the view. We would still love this feature as then we would be able to include the dashboard views in our digital signage system in the office (via Rise Vision running on an Amazon Fire Stick).

Is Dashboard broadcasting in the development pipeline or no?

posts.author: Danny (DIGITAL STEAM)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks
I agree about the number of votes. Hopefully more will vote soon.
Danny

posts.author: Danny (DIGITAL STEAM)
posts.cookedHtml:
Here’s a few pet peeves with the embed/broadcast view
1- Visitors can’t copy data …
2- It’s not super responsive
3- There’s no formatting options (like hide title)
More to come but this is what I had time to write down now
Danny

posts.author: Orhan (Oren)
posts.cookedHtml:
Need to have ability to show mirrored columns on Broadcast view!!!
Or is there a way to have an automation when status changes on one board a status changes on another board?

posts.author: Danny (DIGITAL STEAM)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday
I’m back
Was wondering if this feature request is anywhere up in your priorities ?
Re: Better embed options for boards into external websites
We really (I mean REALLY) would like to formatting options with this
Maybe offer Wordpress or Jooma plugins ?
Thanks for considering and letting us know
Danny

posts.author: Joelburden (Joel Burden)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Totally agree on the CSS functionality.

We would love to display data from the form to password protected client pages, so that they are aware of our progress on certain issues.

If we could have customisation on which columns to show, with the ability to define our own CSS, that would be such an obvious and powerful integration.

Thanks for your update with this!

posts.author: tara (tara)
posts.cookedHtml: Still waiting to hear about updates about this

posts.author: johnnaccarato (John)
posts.cookedHtml:
To create a beautiful board with mirrored information that we control, then not be able to share that view on the internet with others seems silly.

We know what info is being shared, can the columns we don’t want shared, but if we want ALL of the columns shared there should be the option for that.

Please enable this function… it would truly be great!

posts.author: StuartB (Stuart Bowlin)
posts.cookedHtml:
The suggested workaround for this issue: filter and share via link doesn’t always work either- for example, if your filter is by a connect or mirror column, it won’t work, and all of your items will be visible to the public link (yikes!)

For me, my connect column is the “Client-order”, such as ‘2021Q4_ClientName’; and the items are commodity items we sell to many clients, combined on one board. Monday works great to have the orders on one board, showing fulfillment status. I’d say this is the proper way to set up an orders board, no?

Not being able to give clients order breakdowns as the order progresses is a big issue. Since we (admins) can preview what is shown on the public link, we should be able to break any inter-board permissions.

Maybe non-admins shouldn’t be able to share the data, sure. That would give them a way around their permissions, but admins are effectively asking to be able to change the permissions through the share link of all visible items.

posts.author: Noall (Noall)
posts.cookedHtml: 4 years later and this still isn’t a thing.

posts.author: JustinSeed (Justin Seed)
posts.cookedHtml: Make that 6 years!
